title: Publisher Confirms, Windows, and Ambiguity · Execution Trace: Confirm Channel Contract
summary: A Go 1.26 service team must enable confirms before publishing, drain confirmation channels, bound outstanding sequence numbers, and reconcile close ambiguity; reviewers reject hidden goroutine ownership, unbounded work, stale channel state, and claims that stop at a nil client error. The learner predicts values, ownership transitions, and stop paths before seeing a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the confirm channel contract increment in a bounded Go confirm-window ledger. Learners prog…
objectives: Enable confirm mode before publication, continuously consume confirmations, and account for ack, nack, return, close, and unresolved sequence state.

Choose mandatory returns, alternate exchanges, or both from publisher feedback, broker custody, operational ownership, and observability needs; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Predict Confirm Channel Contract · Theory Confirm Channel Contract
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go messaging team handles case 3333 during consumer shutdown drill with one blocked handler goroutine. Build deterministic Go evidence to bound outstanding confirms and preserve ambiguity when a channel closes before proof; keep message identity out of metric labels while preserving causal traces; then raise a broker resource alarm and halt new admission. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Enable confirm mode before publication, continuously consume confirmations, and account for ack, nack, return, close, and unresolved sequence state.
